short_summary: Artificial marks feigned emotion and therefore moves toward fake or sham rather than neutral manufacture.
notes: Edition review still useful before public quotation.

source_name: Thoughts on the Education of Daughters
short_summary: Artificial manners are opposed to genuine feeling and sincerity.

phrase_or_context: artificial as made by art and not natural plus feigned not genuine
short_summary: The same entry contains both made by art and feigned or not genuine senses which supports a trajectory model.
Artificial can simply mean human made and non natural with no sham implication.
Food context links artificial to additives and trust concerns rather than to direct counterfeiting.
Absence claim shows artificial as a negative consumer marker while still not making it a synonym of fake.
notes: Verify page image if quoted later.

short_summary: Regulation normalizes artificial as an industrial category for flavor and color labeling.
Official labeling discourse treats artificial as a live consumer contrast term.
notes: Good modern evidence of negative charge around additives.

short_summary: An artificial object can be realistic by resembling the natural or real while remaining artificial.
Modern usage highlights lifelike resemblance and practicality rather than deception.
Imitation foregrounds copied resemblance and a genuine reference object.
notes: Shows why imitation is closer to substitute than to neutral manufacture.

short_summary: Simulated often means modeled or staged rather than simply human made.
Natural directly opposes artificial in origin and food processing discourse.
Real directly opposes artificial counterfeit and imitation terms in authenticity uses.
